reasoning: The informal statement says "there are infinitely many primes congruent to -1 mod 4". The Lean statement uses the standard encoding of "infinitely many" as: ∀ N, ∃ p ≥ N with the property (this is equivalent to the set being infinite/unbounded in ℕ). The condition "p ≡ -1 (mod 4)" cannot be written literally in ℕ, but `p + 1 ≡ 0 [MOD 4]` (i.e., `Nat.ModEq 4 (p+1) 0`, meaning `(p+1) % 4 = 0`) is precisely equivalent to `p ≡ -1 (mod 4)` (i.e., `p ≡ 3 (mod 4)`). `Nat.Prime p` correctly captures primality. The formalization is a faithful, standard translation.
